description:
Residual cleanup after the .11.24 typer reintegration arc. None of these block other work; this ticket exists so the dead code is tracked rather than left to drift.

Current state: ~81 build warnings, almost all dead-code. Three distinct clusters survived the .24.* closures:

## 1. `src/typer.rs` orphan helpers (~12 warnings)

After .24.1 salvaged the algebra and removed the AST-shaped driver, the file is 300 lines of pure helpers that no consumer references in the live pipeline:

- `expr_calls_self`
- `pattern_type`, `pattern_bindings`, `extract`
- `expr_to_map_key`, `pattern_to_map_key`
- `widen`, `widen_map_sig`, `widen_map`, `widen_tuple`, `widen_list`, `widen_func`

Each is AST-shaped (`&Expr` / `&Pattern`). `ir_typer.rs` doesn't use them. Decision per helper: delete, or move into `types.rs` if the algebra is genuinely useful as a library function. `widen*` in particular may have IR-side analogues worth keeping under a different signature — check `ir_typer::specialize_return` which mentions widen at K=3 (`ir_typer.rs:456` comment).

## 2. `src/value.rs` carcass

`Value::Jit` and `Value::JitPoly` variants never constructed; `JitFn.fn_ptr` and `JitPolyFn.fallback` never read; `BitString::empty` / `from_bytes`, `FzVec::kind_str`, `FzMap::len` unused. The live representation is `fz_value::FzValue` — `value.rs` is the legacy enum.

Decide: delete `value.rs` entirely (and any remaining references), or keep specific variants behind `#[allow(dead_code)]` with a written reason. Default expectation: delete.

## 3. `src/types.rs` printer / DNF infrastructure (~46 warnings)

The set-theoretic core (`Descr` constructors, union/intersect/neg, emptiness) IS used by `ir_typer`. What's unused:

- All `format_*` functions (`format_lit_set`, `format_tuple`, `format_list`, `format_arrow`, `format_map`, `format_map_key`, `format_tuple_clause`, `format_list_clause`, `format_arrow_clause`, `format_map_clause`, `join_clause`)
- `dnf_neg`, `merge_clauses`, `neg_clause`, `is_dnf_top`
- Struct-level "never constructed" warnings on `Conj`, `TupleSig`, `ListSig`, `ArrowSig`, `MapSig`, `LiteralSet`, `Descr`, `BasicBits`, `F64Bits`, `Memo` — these are built indirectly via `Descr` constructors, but no code path builds them as standalone values
- Type aliases `TypeEnv`, `StrSet`, `IntSet`, `FloatSet`, `AtomSet`

The printers were presumably built for diagnostics. `collect_diagnostics` in `ir_typer.rs` produces structured `Diagnostic` values, not formatted strings — so the printers are unhooked. Either wire them into diagnostic rendering (where? probably the `cm.warnings()` path in `ir_codegen`), or drop them.

The `dnf_neg` / neg_clause helpers may be needed when `Descr::diff` is added (currently absent from the live API surface, based on the warning set). Worth confirming with the .24.* design before deletion.

## Smaller leftovers (~9 warnings)

- `src/ast.rs`: `Expr::Dot` and `Expr::Cond` variants never constructed
- `src/ir_codegen.rs`: unused `signed_b`, `fn_ids` locals; unused `Tag`/`Endian` imports; one unnecessary `unsafe` block
- `src/repl.rs`, `src/bitstr.rs`: one warning each

## Deliverable

- A single PR that takes the warning count materially down. Target: under 10 build warnings, each remaining one annotated with `#[allow(dead_code)]` + a one-line reason, or deleted.
- No behavioral change. If a deletion would remove a public API that's documented as "available for future use," call it out in the PR description and decide explicitly.

## Non-goals

- Reviving `src/jit.rs` / `src/aot.rs` / `src/test_support.rs` — those remain cfg-disabled per .11.9; out of scope here.
- Adding new typer features — purely a cleanup pass.

comments.text:
SCOPE UPDATE — type-rendering wire-up moved.

Section 3 of this ticket originally proposed wiring `Descr: Display` (and its `format_*` helpers in `src/types.rs`) into diagnostic messages, or deleting them as cruft. After investigation (2026-05-11) that work has been relocated to **fz-ul4.20.8**, which is part of the new fz-ul4.20 diagnostic-infrastructure arc. The printers are no longer cruft — they're load-bearing for type-driven warning messages once spans flow through the pipeline.

What remains in .11.25 scope:
1. `src/typer.rs` orphan helpers (12 warnings, 300 lines) — pure helpers no consumer references. Delete or migrate into `types.rs` if reusable.
2. `src/value.rs` carcass — `Value::Jit`/`JitPoly`, dead BitString/FzVec methods.
3. Smaller leftovers: `Expr::Dot`/`Cond` variants never constructed (`src/ast.rs`); unused locals/imports in `src/ir_codegen.rs`; one warning each in `src/repl.rs` / `src/bitstr.rs`.

The remaining `dnf_neg` / `merge_clauses` / `neg_clause` / `is_dnf_top` helpers in `types.rs:712-744` are staged for a future `Descr::diff` operation; **keep them**, add a one-line `#[allow(dead_code)]` with a comment pointing at the future ticket, and reduce the warning count via that path. Do NOT delete them.

title: Post-.11.24 cleanup: prune typer/value/types dead code
